expectations.text: Raw tasks from raw-tasks.md are normalized into structured subtasks before execution
expectations.evidence: execution-summary.md states: 'The raw tasks were normalized into structured subtasks with clear goals, change areas, and completion criteria.' execution-status.md shows two structured subtasks (ST-1: Add health check endpoint, ST-2: Add request logging middleware) with IDs, statuses, wave assignments, and review cycle tracking. task-completion-log.md shows each subtask with structured fields including completion criteria verification. Although no raw-tasks.md source file is present in the output directory, the output artifacts clearly demonstrate that raw input was decomposed into structured subtasks.

expectations.text: Health check endpoint implementation exists (handler returning JSON status)
expectations.evidence: internal/health/handler.go contains HandleHealth function that sets Content-Type to application/json, writes HTTP 200, and encodes JSON response with {"status":"ok","version":"1.0.0"}. The response struct has proper json tags. Test file handler_test.go verifies status code, content type, and JSON body parsing.

expectations.text: Health check route is registered in main.go
expectations.evidence: main.go line 35: r.Get("/health", health.HandleHealth) — the health package is imported at line 9 and the route is registered on the chi router.

expectations.text: Logging middleware exists in internal/middleware/logging.go or similar path
expectations.evidence: File exists at codebase/internal/middleware/logging.go containing the RequestLogger function and a responseWriter wrapper type for capturing status codes.

expectations.text: Middleware logs method, path, status code, and duration
expectations.evidence: logging.go line 38: log.Printf("%s %s %d %s", r.Method, r.URL.Path, rw.statusCode, duration) — all four fields are logged. The test file logging_test.go independently verifies method (POST/GET), path (/test/path, /default), and status code (201, 200) are present in log output. Duration is computed via time.Since(start).

expectations.text: Middleware is applied to the router in main.go
expectations.evidence: main.go line 32: r.Use(middleware.RequestLogger) — the middleware package is imported at line 10 and RequestLogger is applied to the chi router before any route registrations.

expectations.text: Task review was performed for each task
expectations.evidence: task-completion-log.md records for each subtask: 'Task review: passed on attempt 1'. execution-status.md shows 'Review Cycles: 1' for both ST-1 and ST-2. execution-summary.md has an Acceptance Criteria Verification table with 7 criteria all marked 'met' and verified by task review.

expectations.text: Code review was performed for each task
expectations.evidence: task-completion-log.md records for each subtask: 'Code review: passed on attempt 1'. execution-summary.md states '100% of subtasks passed both reviews on first attempt' and notes 'Code consistently followed existing patterns (handlers in internal packages, standard library usage, chi middleware conventions).'

File exists at outputs/execution-status.md with full tracking content: task description, subtask table (ID, Title, Status, Wave, Review Cycles, Notes), status summary table, wave progress, recently completed items, blocked items section, and escalations section.
